name: Source Fidelity appraisal for Tad Mood Wellbeing Quality Life bundle
summary: Accepted. Bibliographic metadata, PMIDs, DOIs, sample counts, interventions, endpoints, and abstract-level quantitative results match the accessed PubMed, Nature abstract, and NCBI E-utilities records. The staged bundle correctly marks Seftel, Porst, and Brock as abstract-level sources and does not make detailed full-text-only claims from them; Choi and ETLAS-2 have PMC full-text links but the first-pass extraction remains tied to abstract-verifiable results.

findings.claim_or_issue: The four new source records carry located PubMed metadata, PMIDs, DOIs where available, author lists, and source summaries; ETLAS-2 and DailyMed are reused live sources.
findings.why_it_matters: The claim support map depends on specific ED psychosocial, ED/depression, BPH/LUTS quality-of-life, CSVD mental-well-being, and safety sources.

findings.claim_or_issue: Seftel 2009, Porst 2012, and Brock 2013 are extracted from abstract-level records in this first baseline.
findings.why_it_matters: Abstract-level extraction supports the broad direction and directness boundary but should not be used for detailed SEAR subdomains, adverse-event tables, or subgroup analyses.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the Seftel full-text follow-up open and retrieve full reports before quantitative synthesis or detailed subdomain comparison.
